entries.title: Post-All Might Rankings Create A Symbol Vacuum

context.label: JP Hero Billboard Chart establishes the post-All Might rankings

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The JP Hero Billboard Chart formalizes a post-All Might hero order, but it cannot instantly replace the Symbol of Peace.
content.injection: When the chart appears, show institutional ranking without emotional replacement. Society still lacks All Might’s stabilizing symbol.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: Endeavor Is Number One But Not Loved Like All Might

context.label: Endeavor stands publicly as the new Number One Hero

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Endeavor becomes the Number One Hero by rank, but he does not inherit All Might’s public warmth, moral symbolism, or trust automatically.
content.injection: Write Endeavor’s No. 1 role as burdensome and contested. He has status, but not All Might’s emotional legitimacy.

entries.title: Hawks Is Number Two And Too Fast For Normal Expectations

context.label: Hawks appears as the young Number Two Hero

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Hawks is introduced as the young Number Two Hero, famous for speed, efficiency, and a relaxed public persona.
content.injection: Use Hawks as fast, sharp, and socially disarming. His casual tone must not mean he is careless.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

ability.name: Fierce Wings
constraints: Feathers can move independently and sense vibration, but they are finite and can be damaged.
antiLore: Do not treat Hawks as a brute-force brawler; his strength is speed, rescue, precision, and information.

entries.title: Hero Rankings Are Public Performance As Well As Merit

context.label: JP Hero Billboard Chart establishes the post-All Might rankings

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Hero rankings reflect public approval, incident resolution, and visibility as well as combat ability.
content.injection: Use rankings as social machinery. A ranked hero is managing reputation, trust, and media meaning, not only raw battle power.

entries.title: Hawks Public Persona Masks Hidden Calculation

context.label: Hawks appears as the young Number Two Hero

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Hawks’s relaxed public persona hides political intelligence, calculation, and obligations that are not visible to most characters.
content.injection: Write Hawks with a split surface: casual and irreverent outside, observant and mission-conscious underneath.

entries.title: Endeavor Wants To Define His Own Symbol

context.label: Endeavor stands publicly as the new Number One Hero

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Endeavor is forced to confront what kind of Number One Hero he can be without copying All Might.
content.injection: In Endeavor scenes, frame his burden as definition: he must become a symbol on different terms, under public and family scrutiny.

entries.title: Todoroki Family Pressure Shadows Endeavor Ranking

context.label: Todoroki family processes Endeavor’s public stand

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Endeavor’s public rise is shadowed by his abusive family history and the unresolved harm within the Todoroki household.
content.injection: Do not write Endeavor’s rank as redemption by itself. His family harm remains active context even when he performs heroically.

content.fact: Hawks deliberately engages Endeavor in public and strategic partnership around the Nomu threat.
content.injection: Let Hawks prod Endeavor while also guiding optics and intelligence flow. The partnership is useful but not simple friendship.

entries.title: Nomu Rumors Signal Organized Villain Evolution

content.fact: Nomu rumors indicate that villain bioengineering and organization remain active after Kamino and Hassaikai.
content.injection: In Pro Hero arc scenes, treat Nomu as a continuing organized threat, not a solved USJ/Kamino relic.

entries.title: Hero Billboard Chart Creates Pressure On Top Ten

context.label: JP Hero Billboard Chart establishes the post-All Might rankings

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The Billboard Chart places symbolic pressure on top-ranked heroes to reassure society in All Might’s absence.
content.injection: Use the chart as pressure distribution: the top heroes are not merely celebrated, they are expected to stabilize the public.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

context.label: Endeavor stands publicly as the new Number One Hero

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Endeavor struggles with public warmth and symbolic gestures because his hero identity was built around surpassing All Might, not comforting society.
content.injection: Write Endeavor as forceful and awkward with public reassurance. His effort matters because it does not come naturally.
